setting.note: The Cameron Highlands are Malaysia's cool tea country, a hill station of mist, jungle and terraced estates high in Pahang. A survivor of a wartime Japanese camp comes here to build a garden for her dead sister and instead apprentices to a Japanese gardener, while the Malayan Emergency simmers in the surrounding forest. The map runs from the town of Tanah Rata out across the tea slopes to the mossy summit forest, with the frame narrator's Kuala Lumpur far below.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Tan Twan Eng, first published 2012. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The Garden of Evening Mists, Tan Twan Eng (2012). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The green of the tea terraces and the highland jungle that frame the story. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
